Ad Text:
Work with cutting edge AI technology, making the world a safer and more secure place. DroneShield (ASX:DRO) offers an opportunity to solve some of world’s most challenging technical problems in the rapidly growing counter-drone sector.   

Our customers operate in some of the most challenging and high-stakes environments in the world, including military organisations, government agencies, airports, critical infrastructure operators, and law enforcement. Protecting airspace in these settings requires technology that performs under pressure and teams that understand what’s at stake. At DroneShield, employees work at the leading edge of counter-drone innovation, helping to address real-world security challenges as drone threats continue to evolve globally. 

With one of the largest listed defence company market capitalisations in Australia, now part of the ASX200 index, DroneShield is experiencing a period of hypergrowth. Revenue has surged from A$57 million in 2024 to over A$190 million in 2025, representing growth of more than 400% year-on-year, with record profitability and cashflow. The total addressable global market for counter-drone is assessed at approximately $100 billion, and is currently at the nascent stage with much of the growth still to come, with DroneShield well positioned as a global market leader, and the only public listed pure-play business in this sector.   

The company has grown from 11 employees in 2017 to over 450 staff globally today, and is on track to reach around 550 by the end of 2026. This expansion includes investment of over A$50 million annually in R&D, a global pipeline exceeding A$2.5 billion, and continuous scaling of production capacity to meet accelerating demand.  







About the role

DroneShield is seeking an Algorithms Software Engineer with relevant experience to join the team in Adelaide, South Australia. The position will report to the Director of Engineering, Sensors and Effectors.

The Sensors and Effectors team at Droneshield is focused on providing fully operational and qualified modular devices to enable new product development of high-end RF based sensors and effectors. These modules are re-used across various product configurations including man-portable, vehicle mounted and fixed site. Key technical challenges for the electronics aspect of these modules include the design of complex wide-band RF circuitry, high speed digital interface and high-power radio frequency output. DroneShield designs and builds the full signal chain from signal generation to antenna.

The primary focus of the role is to research and develop DroneShield’s next generation of Capabilities, working closely with ML/AI, Data Science, RF and infrastructure teams to innovate and enhance DroneShield’s world leading Counter-Drone technology. It represents an exceptional opportunity for a result-driven individual in a new niche but exploding market.

Responsibilities, Duties and Expectations 

Take drone detection algorithms from design to production using C++, working closely with a team of Algorithm and Software Engineers
Develop and maintain simulation software used to assist algorithm development
Design and implement data analysis and algorithm evaluation pipelines
Conduct unit testing to ensure software quality and reliability
Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines
Assist software & hardware development teams to make informed decisions about the future direction of complex deployed system design
Advance the development of software architecture design processes within the team. Ensure software is designed for reliability, and performance on resource-constrained targets

Qualifications, Experience and Skills 

Bachelor’s (or higher) degree in Computer Science, Electronics Engineering, a similar technical field of study, or equivalent practical experience
7+ years of software development experience (preferred: Python, C++)
Ability to work in a multidisciplinary team, including communicating effectively with engineers from non-software / data science backgrounds
Ability to turn complex algorithms and mathematical models into high-performance software, operating on sensor data
Working knowledge of modern architectural patterns and software design pattern

Experience with the following:

At least one compiled language (Go, C++, or C)
At least one scripting language (Python, JavaScript, or Shell)
Digital Signal Processing concepts and techniques
Embedded Linux platforms
Test Driven Development
Revision Control
Continuous Integration

Who you are

You are comfortable deploying state of the art algorithmic models to deliver commercial value.
You are a lifelong learner. You’re self-taught and continuously learning. You are interested in keeping up with current best practices in your areas of expertise.
You are experienced working with modern infrastructure and tools.
You are comfortable running your software in Linux environments.
An abundance of ambition and motivation to grow the company and compete with the big players in the industry.

Note for recruitment agencies: We do not accept unsolicited candidates from external recruiters unless specifically instructed.

Job description:




Senior Data Engineer | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Senior Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Advanced degrees or relevant certifications in Azure or Databricks are a plus.

Desirable: Knowledge of Azure Fabric and emerging data engineering technologies.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating, and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - up, into new areas, or across the globe.

We embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and cultivate inclusion, making our work environment a space where everyone belongs.

We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave, and a return-to-work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

Nuage has partnered exclusively with a Melbourne-based enterprise to secure a Senior AI Engineer for one of the most technically progressive internal AI teams in the market. This is an opportunity to join a small, high-calibre SWAT team responsible for building production-grade GenAI solutions that are already shaping how the wider business operates.

This is not a role focused on experimentation alone. The team is delivering real enterprise AI platforms end-to-end, with engineers who are trusted to own architecture, design, and hands-on delivery from concept through to production. You will work closely with Data Science, Machine Learning, and Data Engineering specialists to solve genuine business problems using modern AI tooling, while having the autonomy to influence technical direction and explore new approaches.

The environment is particularly attractive for engineers who enjoy building properly: strong technical leadership, meaningful ownership, and access to a serious R&D budget for emerging AI technologies.

What they are looking for:

Proven enterprise GenAI build experience

Strong AWS depth across MLOps and Data Engineering services

Exposure to Snowflake or Databricks

Strong software engineering capability (Python ideally)

Experience leading end-to-end technical delivery

This is a hybrid Melbourne CBD role, suited to engineers who want to work at the forefront of enterprise AI in a team where technical standards are genuinely high. If you are looking for a role where you can build modern AI systems properly, this is worth exploring

VALD is the world leader in technology for the allied health industry, providing innovative human-measurement technology to over 10,000 clients in over 100 countries. If you have a favorite team in the NBA, EPL, or NFL, there's a good chance they use VALD Technologies.

Years on from its humble beginnings in Brisbane, Australia, VALD has grown to a team of over 300 team members in over 30 countries, with five offices across four continents.

Driven by a multidisciplinary team of researchers, clinicians, sports scientists, designers, developers and engineers, VALD’s suite of systems offer unparalleled insight into human movement, performance, injury risk and rehabilitation.

About the role

VALD is building the world’s largest musculoskeletal database, with over two billion data points and counting. Using this data, we can help practitioners to objectively assess patients’ progress and make more accurate, data-informed treatment decisions. We are looking for a data engineer to join our growing team and build the technical foundations for VALD's future growth. 

In this role, you will 

Maintain the health of VALD’s data ingestion and transformation pipelines in Azure Databricks.
Add new data sources, implement business rules, and produce new metrics to provide users with greater insights, supporting key business decisions. 
Work closely with the Product team to develop new data models for VALD’s expanding product suite, following best practices in Kimball dimensional modelling. 
Partner with the Data Science team to build new solutions that extend VALD’s leadership in MSK technology, including normative data serving, secure client data sharing, and MLOps infrastructure. 
Use vendor tools to monitor and improve the data quality and security in the Databricks lakehouse. 
Serve as a data domain expert with an in-depth understanding of our products. 

What we are looking for

Our ideal candidate has: 


Proven data engineering experience with Azure technologies, including Azure Databricks, Azure SQL Database and Azure Data Lake Storage.
Proficiency in Spark SQL, PySpark, and T-SQL programming languages. 
Ability to create semantic models, measures, and dashboards in Power BI, including DirectQuery connections. 
Experience developing CI/CD pipelines and following DevOps best practices. 

Why VALD?

You will be working in a fast-paced, dynamic company that has a culture around the belief that anything is possible with hard work, creativity, dedication, and good timing. 

We have a range of benefits we offer to our team, such as:


The opportunity to work in a company that is redefining allied healthcare. 

Working with a range of high-performing individuals and teams across a range of disciplines. 

Working with contemporary tooling on industry first applications. 

Casual work attire. 

Chefs that prepare you delicious fresh breakfast and lunch. 

An on-site barista to make your coffee just as you like it. 

A team gym, masseuse, and access to physiotherapy and exercise physiology services. 

Diversity & Inclusion Commitment

VALD’s best asset is not our technology but our people and culture. A culture of inclusion and diversity is critical to our business. We know diverse teams perform better. It's not a separate initiative – we aim to embed inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We are committed to fostering an inclusive work environment and embracing diversity, including gender, nationality, disability, age, marital/parental status, ethnicity, gender identity, socioeconomic background and sexual orientation. We welcome applications from people from all backgrounds.

Apply now

In your cover letter, please share with us why you think your experience is relevant to this role and how this opportunity fits into your longer-term career goals. We can't wait to meet you and discuss what a career with VALD could look like for you!

Successful applicants will be subject to background checks (including identity and criminal record checks). It will be a condition of employment that the background checks return acceptable results.

AAMC are looking for an Integration Developer!



The Role

This 6 month Contract role is an excellent opportunity for an experienced integration developer with a passion for cloud technologies to use their skills to deliver tangible value for the business as we continue our journey of continuous improvement in operational excellence with our customers and suppliers.

As an Integration Developer, you will play an integral role implementing new integration solutions here at AAMC which we have planned for 2026.

This role will focus on building scalable, secure and maintainable services and APIs in Python, so it is important that you are fluent in that language to hit the ground running.

Here at AAMC we have a robust secure development lifecycle to ensure solutions are deployed into production in a secure manner and offer industry leading security tools to support you during the design and implementation of your code.

Reporting to our Integration & Automation Lead, you will have the opportunity to contribute to CI/CD pipelines, develop unit, integration and e2e automated tests.

You will also collaborate with technical and business stakeholders to ensure solutions are fit for purpose and meet the needs of our clients and wider service providers.

Due to the nature of our integrations, an aptitude for troubleshooting and resolving complex issues across multiple systems in a timely manner is critical to being successful in this role.

As a fully remote National organisation, this role is entirely a work from home position. It is important that you are comfortable working as a member of a remote team.




About You

5+ years of experience in integration of data across systems

Strong proficiency in Python

Experience building and consuming APIs

Experience building serverless functions that interact with a variety of downstream services

Experience deploying and managing applications in Azure, ideally using Azure DevOps

Sound knowledge of Azure services including Azure Functions, Azure Container Apps, Cosmos DB, Logic Apps

About Us

Kempe Engineering is a privately owned, global specialist engineering company headquartered in Geelong, Victoria. We provide maintenance, modernisation, and process equipment products and services across aluminium smelting, major resources, and other heavy industries. With branches nationally and internationally, we work in long-term relationships to develop and add value to our clients’ capital assets.

The Role

We’re looking for an AI Project Manager to lead our ERP automation programme. This is a greenfield build — you’ll take the strategy off the page and into production, leading an internal team from day one and reporting directly to the Operations Director.

What You’ll Do

•       Design, develop, and deploy RPA and AI-driven automation across our AX2009 ERP environment.

•       Lead master data cleansing and enrichment across ~38,000 SKUs to achieve automation-ready standards.

•       Oversee the consolidation of business data and IP into a structured environment with an AI overlay for search, reporting, and management dashboards.

•       Mentor and build capability within the AI Systems team through hands-on delivery and code review.

•       Establish automation standards, documentation, and governance frameworks.

•       Track KPIs and continuously refine automations based on analytics and business feedback.

What You’ll Bring

•       5+ years in RPA, AI, or ERP automation project leadership.

•       Proven experience delivering automation within legacy ERP environments (Dynamics AX or equivalent).

•       Ability to evaluate and implement tooling across RPA, AI/ML, and data integration.

•       Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Nice to Have

•       Certification in UiPath, Power Platform, or equivalent.

•       Experience with AI/ML model development or data science tooling.

•       Background in manufacturing, engineering services, or supply chain.

Why Kempe

This isn’t an innovation lab experiment. You’ll be building production automation across a real operating business with a clear commercial mandate. The strategy is funded, the team is in place, and you’ll have the autonomy to shape how we deliver.

How to Apply

Click ‘Apply’ and submit your CV with a brief cover letter.

About the Role 

We are seeking an AI Engineer to design, build, and integrate advanced AI capabilities into enterprise‑grade software solutions. This role focuses on implementing AI-driven features within a large web‑based platform, working collaboratively with product, data science, and engineering teams.

Key Responsibilities



Design and implement AI solutions within software products, including generative and agentic AI features.
Contribute to the design and integration of AI components within existing software architectures.
Build and deploy AI-enabled features in cloud‑native environments (e.g., AWS, GCP).
Work closely with product and data science teams to ensure AI solutions align with business objectives and compliance requirements.
Contribute to engineering best practices through high‑quality code, documentation, and collaboration.
Implement and support evaluation and monitoring mechanisms to ensure AI solutions meet accuracy and reliability standards.
Requirements 

Australian Citizenship (mandatory for NV1 defence clearance eligibility).
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
3–5+ years’ experience in software engineering or technology development, with hands‑on experience designing and implementing AI solutions.
Experience working with generative AI and agentic AI systems in production or near‑production environments.
Solid understanding of software engineering principles, data storage, and cloud platforms.
Strong collaboration, problem‑solving, and communication skills, including technical documentation (e.g., UML).
Familiarity with web technologies and frameworks (React, Spring) is advantageous.
About Fivecast

Fivecast is a world-leading provider of cutting-edge digital intelligence solutions. We enable the world’s most important public and private organizations to explore masses of data and uncover actionable insights, critical to protecting global communities.

As a growing tech company with over 150 people globally, all team members are provided with the opportunity to directly contribute to our success, participate in the development of new and emerging technologies and grow their career in a challenging and dynamic high-tech environment.  We are headquartered in Adelaide, Australia with teams in Canberra, Virginia USA, and London UK.

Our Work Environment and Culture 

Our Adelaide based headquarters is a purpose-built tech hub centrally based in vibrant Hutt Street. We moved into this brand-new space in early 2023 to allow for continued collaboration, innovation & growth.
  
Our space has been specifically engineered for brainstorming, idea generation, random encounters across teams and to suit our casual, relaxed culture. This has been achieved through amazing features, including an arcade room, pool table, fully equipped kitchens with fruit and snacks, barista quality coffee machine, end of trip facilities (showers, lockers, bike storage, etc), as well as comfortable breakout spaces.

Fivecast has a connected work culture with team members taking advantage of our social clubs, local cafes, bars, pubs and sporting facilities for team activities. At Fivecast we understand the importance of a good work-life balance and support flexible work arrangements.

Fivecast is an inclusive employer, we value diversity of thought and experience and believe our inclusive culture contributes to our success. We are committed to building a workforce that is representative of the communities in which we live and work.
Additional information

Join a dynamic high-tech environment
Progression opportunities and share options
Amazing people, fun culture & a pool table

About the Role

At ICMS, learning is designed with purpose, grounded in academic quality, shaped by industry, and evolving with the world our graduates will enter. 

As artificial intelligence reshapes Industry and higher education, we are seeking an experienced, forward‑thinking Learning Designer (AI and Assessment) to help lead this transformation and ensure our subjects remain rigorous, relevant, and future‑focused. This is a rare opportunity to lead assessment led subject (re)development, influence institutional practice, and work at the intersection of pedagogy, industry relevance, and emerging technologies.

Why this role matters
Assessment drives learning and, at ICMS, we are rethinking assessment to ensure it is:

authentic and industry focused
academically rigorous and secure
transparent and ethical in the use of AI
aligned to programmatic assessment and constructive alignment

In this role, you will lead the redesign of subjects from the ground up, using backwards curriculum design to ensure learning outcomes, assessment, activities, and content work together to support meaningful student learning.

What you’ll be doing
As a Learning Designer, you will:

Lead subject redevelopment from concept to implementation, ensuring high quality, contemporary and assessment led learning design
Design and transform authentic, industry aligned assessments, embedding clear guidance on AI use and academic integrity
Champion the ethical and responsible use of AI in assessment design, feedback, and learning activities
Ensure quality, consistency and compliance across subjects, aligned to ICMS frameworks, AQF requirements, Universal Design for Learning, and regulatory standards
Collaborate with academic leaders and teaching staff to facilitate design workshops, support capability building, and drive continuous improvement through feedback and learning analytics

About you

You are a learning design professional who is excited by the challenge of improving assessment and learning in a complex, real world higher education environment. You bring:
A postgraduate qualification in education, learning design, or a related field (or equivalent professional experience)
Strong expertise in course and subject (re)development
Deep understanding of assessment design and backwards curriculum design
Proven experience integrating AI considerations into assessment and learning design
Confidence working with learning technologies and LMS platforms (including Moodle)
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
A collaborative mindset and a passion for educational quality and innovation

Why join ICMS?

Be part of a values driven institution focused on educational excellence, employability, and innovation
Work with engaged academic leaders and a supportive Learning & Teaching team
Influence institutional practice at a time of significant transformation in assessment and AI
Enjoy working across our iconic Manly campus and City campus, in a collegial and forward looking environment

How to apply

Please apply with your CV and cover letter/position statement that outlines your interest in the role, how you meet the requirements and examples of relevant work demonstrating your approach to assessment led design and AI enabled learning via this recruitment portal. 

Important: Current ICMS staff should apply via ELMO / Careers / Internal Vacancies.

There is no closing date associated with this role. Your early application is encouraged.

Further information

Explore here how ICMS integrates AI in Education: 

https://policies.icms.edu.au/artificial-intelligence-in-education-aied-framework/
https://policies.icms.edu.au/use-of-artificial-intelligence-ai-in-assessment-guidelines/

Link to the Job Description

https://www.icms.edu.au/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/JD-Learning-Designer-AI-and-Assessments.pdf

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

As per the Institution's Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Policy, ICMS aims to provide an accessible, inclusive and collaborative learning and working environment that promotes fairness, equity, respect for social and cultural diversity, and that is free from discrimination and harassment.

Job Title: Survey Systems Engineer – LiDAR & Field Operations
Ad Text:
Role: Survey Systems Engineer – LiDAR & Field Operations
Location: Melbourne, VIC
Employment Type: Full-time Permanent

Role Overview

We are seeking a Survey Systems Engineer – LiDAR & Field Operations to provide hands-on technical expertise across the design, deployment, operation, and maintenance of advanced survey and data acquisition systems. This role combines engineering, surveying, and field operations, supporting the capture and processing of high-quality LiDAR and imagery data across aerial, rail, and road-based platforms.

The role is highly practical and field-oriented, requiring close collaboration with project managers, surveyors, and clients to ensure survey systems operate reliably and data is delivered accurately and on schedule.

Key Responsibilities
Survey Systems & Engineering

Provide technical expertise for the design, configuration, operation, and maintenance of survey systems.

Assemble and integrate surveying equipment components, including lighting, wiring, localisation, and power systems.

Design and implement real-time field surveying systems within appropriate electrical engineering and surveying frameworks.

Undertake light fabrication, wiring, and installation of instrumentation and power supplies as required.

Field Data Acquisition & Operations

Perform operational duties related to LiDAR and imagery acquisition, including:

Aerial capture using helicopter and fixed-wing aircraft

Rail-based survey platforms

Road-based and mobile mapping vehicles

Support field deployments by ensuring systems are correctly installed, calibrated, and operational.

Assist with the planning and coordination of field data acquisition activities to meet project timelines.

Data Quality & Processing Support

Ensure all planned data acquisition activities are conducted accurately, safely, and efficiently.

Assist with quality control and validation of acquired LiDAR, imagery, and survey data.

Support the processing and management of collected spatial data, including preliminary GIS and spatial workflows.

Stakeholder & Project Support

Provide technical advice and input to project teams throughout the project lifecycle.

Liaise with project managers and customer representatives to support delivery objectives.

Assist with troubleshooting system performance issues and implementing technical improvements.

Skills & Experience
Essential

Experience working with survey systems, LiDAR, imagery capture, or mobile mapping platforms.

Practical skills in electrical systems, instrumentation, wiring, and system integration.

Experience supporting field-based data acquisition activities.

Understanding of surveying and geospatial data principles.

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work effectively in field environments.

Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders.

Desirable

Background in Electrical, Mechatronics, Systems, or Geospatial Engineering, Surveying, or Spatial Science.

Experience with GIS or spatial data processing tools.

Exposure to aerial, rail, or mobile LiDAR operations.

Experience working on infrastructure, transport, or government projects.

Key Attributes

Hands-on and technically curious

Comfortable working both in the field and office environments

Strong attention to detail and data quality

Adaptable and able to respond to dynamic field conditions

Collaborative and client-focused

Enterprise Integration Developer
Connect Legacy Systems to AI-Powered Workflows

Galileo Search
Sydney / Contract / Hybrid

Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Every enterprise runs on systems that were built before AI existed — SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Oracle, custom middleware, legacy databases, and a tangle of integrations that nobody fully understands. Now these companies want Claude to work with all of it. Somebody needs to build the bridges.
You're the bridge builder.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. Enterprise Integration Developers are the specialists who connect Claude to the systems companies already run — not in a lab, not as a proof of concept, but in production, at scale, with real data flowing through real business processes.
  
What you'll do on client engagements

Build production integrations between Claude/Cowork and enterprise platforms — SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Oracle, Microsoft 365, SharePoint, and custom internal systems
Develop MCP servers and connectors that give Claude secure, structured access to enterprise data and functionality
Design and implement middleware that translates between legacy system formats and Claude's API — handling authentication, data transformation, error handling, and retry logic
Build event-driven integrations that trigger Claude workflows from enterprise system events — new ticket created, invoice received, employee onboarded, contract signed
Work with Solutions Architects to implement integration patterns that are maintainable, scalable, and secure
Debug integration issues across complex enterprise stacks — tracing data flow from source system through middleware to Claude and back
What you bring

5+ years in enterprise integration, middleware development, or systems integration
Deep experience with at least one major enterprise platform — SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics
Strong API development skills — REST, GraphQL, SOAP, webhooks, and event-driven patterns
Integration platform experience — MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure Integration Services, AWS Step Functions, or similar
Programming proficiency in Java, Python, TypeScript, or C# — you write production code that handles edge cases and failures gracefully
Understanding of enterprise security requirements — OAuth, SAML, certificate-based auth, API gateway patterns
Bonus (not required)

Experience with Claude API, OpenAI API, or other LLM integration
MuleSoft, Boomi, or equivalent integration platform certification
SAP, Salesforce, or ServiceNow developer certification
Experience with message queues and event streaming (Kafka, RabbitMQ, Azure Service Bus)
Background in mining, energy, financial services, or government enterprise environments
What Galileo offers

Great contract rates reflecting level of experience 
You do the work that makes AI real — without your integrations, Claude is just a chatbot. With them, it's a business system
Free Anthropic certification — understand Claude's capabilities so you can design integrations that leverage them fully
Your existing enterprise platform expertise becomes dramatically more valuable when combined with AI integration skills
Enterprise integration developers who add AI to their toolkit are the most versatile technologists in the market right now. Every company has SAP, Salesforce, or ServiceNow. Every company wants AI. The developers who can connect the two will write their own ticket for the next decade.
  
To apply: Share your CV and showing a summary of the most complex enterprise integration you've built — what systems you connected, what patterns you used, and how you handled the inevitable edge cases. We value production experience over certifications.

Cybersecurity & AI Governance Specialist
Secure Enterprise AI Deployments

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid
Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Deploying AI into an enterprise isn't just a technology decision — it's a security decision. When Claude accesses internal systems via MCP, processes sensitive documents, and generates outputs that employees act on, the attack surface changes. The risk profile changes. The governance requirements change. And most companies haven't caught up yet.

They need someone who has.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic professionals. Cybersecurity & AI Governance Specialists are the people who ensure enterprise Claude deployments meet security standards, comply with regulations, and don't create risks that nobody thought about until it was too late.
  
What you'll do on client engagements

Conduct AI security assessments for enterprise Claude deployments — evaluating prompt injection risks, data exfiltration vectors, access control gaps, and output validation requirements
Design security architecture for MCP connections — ensuring Claude's access to internal systems follows least-privilege principles with comprehensive audit logging
Develop AI governance frameworks aligned with the Australian AI Ethics Principles, NIST AI RMF, and industry-specific regulations (APRA CPS 234, Essential Eight, ISO 27001)
Build AI risk registers and risk treatment plans — identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks specific to large language model deployments
Advise boards and executive leadership on AI risk posture — translating technical security findings into business risk language
Work with Data Governance Leads to ensure data access controls and classification are enforced at the AI layer
Design incident response procedures for AI-specific scenarios — model misuse, data leakage through AI outputs, adversarial attacks
What you bring

7+ years in cybersecurity, information security, or IT risk management
Experience conducting security assessments, penetration testing, or security architecture reviews
Understanding of enterprise security frameworks — ISO 27001, NIST CSF, Essential Eight, CPS 234, or IRAP
Ability to assess novel technology risks — you don't need a playbook for every scenario. You can think from first principles about what could go wrong, and design controls proportionate to the risk
Strong communication skills — you can explain AI security risks to a board audit committee without either terrifying them or boring them
Experience in regulated industries (government, financial services, healthcare, critical infrastructure) is highly valued
Bonus (not required)

CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or equivalent security certification
Experience with AI/ML security risks — adversarial attacks, prompt injection, model poisoning, data poisoning
IRAP assessor status or experience supporting IRAP assessments
Familiarity with Anthropic's security model, Claude's safety features, or LLM security research
Experience with GRC platforms (ServiceNow GRC, Archer, OneTrust)
What Galileo offers

Great contract day rates — reflecting the seniority and impact of the role
You're the person who signs off on security before any Claude deployment goes live — that's authority, not just advisory
Free Anthropic certification — you'll understand Claude's architecture, safety mechanisms, and security model from the inside
Work with Data Governance Leads, Solutions Architects, and Engineering teams who handle implementation while you own risk
First-mover positioning in AI security — the professionals who develop AI governance expertise now will define the standards that every enterprise follows
AI governance is the fastest-growing specialty in cybersecurity. Every enterprise deploying AI needs someone who can bridge traditional InfoSec with the novel risks that LLMs introduce. Every board audit committee is asking about AI risk. Every regulator is developing AI-specific guidance. The security professionals who build this expertise now won't just be in demand — they'll be writing the frameworks everyone else follows.
  
To apply: Share your CV and a brief overview of a security assessment or governance framework you've developed — particularly any work involving emerging technology risks. If you've thought about AI security in any capacity, we want to hear about it.

Job Title: Senior Software Engineer - Java & GCP - Contract
Ad Text:
Would you like to build a high-impact fraud technology function focused on protecting customers through AI-enabled, cloud native platforms? Would you like to join an engineering team tackling complex, real time challenges, designing resilient systems that operate at scale and have a direct impact on customer trust and security?

What You'll Be Working On

Building and evolving mission‑critical fraud detection and prevention platforms used by millions of customers
Designing and developing high‑performance, backend services in Java within a cloud‑native architecture
Deploying and operating scalable microservices on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) using Kubernetes
Leveraging AI and machine learning capabilities 
What We're Looking For

Deep hands‑on expertise with a modern Java stack
Proven experience building and running workloads on Google Cloud Platform
Solid knowledge of Kubernetes and containerised microservices
Experience or strong interest in leveraging AI within software systems (e.g. integrating ML models, using AI services, or building intelligent pipelines)
A background in high‑availability, high‑throughput systems (banking, payments, fraud, fintech, risk-engine's or similar highly regulated domains is a plus)
You'll join a team who work at the intersection of advanced software engineering and AI-driven intelligence, delivering platforms that continuously evolve. 

Duration: 12 months
Location: Melbourne CBD, Hybrid
Rate: $1000 per day

Who you’ll be working with

At AC3, our purpose is to make technology real, and as leaders in secure multi-cloud solutions, we get to bring that purpose to life for our customers every day. We’re an Australian-owned ICT managed service provider with teams in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, and Auckland, supporting more than 700 enterprise organisations and over 50% of the NSW State Government.
  
Why join us?

What sets us apart is our culture - people here have real relationships that go beyond work, and maintaining our awesome culture, supportive spirit, and belief that everyone is heard and treated with respect, is a top priority at AC3.
We’re committed to a workplace that fosters open communication, flexible working arrangements, continuous learning, and providing support to get through life’s changing seasons.
We live by the AC3 way - Work Wise, Work Humble, and Work Honest.
  
Who are we looking for?

As a Platforms Engineer (Compute & Storage), you will be responsible for the planning, provisioning and lifecycle management of AC3’s compute, SAN/NAS storage and backup infrastructure. You’ll drive hardware install/config/validation, break-fix, upgrades, migrations and decommissioning, while continuously improving monitoring, capacity management, automation and documentation. This role partners closely with Cloud Infrastructure, Networking, SRE and Facilities teams and supports escalations across the business.

AC3 is dedicated to grow the representation of women in technology and are warmly encouraged to apply.
  
What you’ll do?

Plan, provision and manage compute and storage infrastructure across AC3: install, configure, validate, break-fix, migrate and decommission.
Perform hardware and OOB/UEFI/middleware upgrades and refreshes; contribute to lifecycle planning and platform health recommendations.
Support capacity management and automate tasks/processes wherever possible; use orchestration tooling where practical.
Maintain and improve service-based monitoring coverage; consolidate physical asset information in a centralised database.
Manage backup infrastructure (Commvault): lifecycle tasks, capacity management, monitoring intelligence and backup success targets.
Work across Platforms teams to support integrations and automation (including HPE DMF where applicable).
Participate as a standby/on-call resource for compute and storage hardware-specific alerts and escalations (as required).
Maintain strong information security and quality practices (policies, training, reporting risks/events). 
What we'd love to see?

Australian citizenship.
Experience working with compute infrastructure across common vendors and lifecycle management of converged/rackmount platforms.
Experience implementing and managing NAS and SAN storage infrastructure and working with storage vendors (HPE, HDS, NetApp or Pure).
Experience with large SAN-level data migrations.
Experience with Commvault and backup infrastructure lifecycle management.
Experience with hypervisors and common operating systems.
Experience with ticketing/collaboration tooling (ServiceNow, Wiki, SharePoint preferred) and maintaining a monitoring platform.
Strong customer service orientation, practical problem solving and ability to prioritise workload.
ITIL Foundation (v3 or later).
Even if you don't check every box, your passion and curiosity matter. If this role excites you, we would love to see your application.
  
A plus if you have…

Experience working in an MSP environment.
Broader platform knowledge across private/public cloud, Linux/Windows, hypervisors, firewalls and networks.
Relevant vendor certifications (HPE, Cisco UCS, IBM, Dell, Lenovo, NetApp, EMC, HDS).

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" AI
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" Agentic AI and Gen AI 


Morgan Consulting are partnering with an exciting and fast growing organisation seeking a Senior Data Engineer to join their Data function. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience. This role offers strong Career Development within an organisation which operates with very little red-tape and has a supportive and positive culture.

About the Role:

The Senior Data Engineer is a hands on technical specialist responsible for building and supporting modern Databricks Lakehouse solutions on Azure within a complex retail data landscape. Working across both project initiatives and BAU, the role delivers robust, scalable data pipelines and curated analytical datasets that underpin enterprise BI, advanced analytics, and AI. You will collaborate closely with retail stakeholders, BI developers, and broader technology teams to transform high volume retail data into trusted, production ready insights that support critical business outcomes. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.

What You Will Do:

Platform & Analytics Enablement

Enable enterprise BI, analytics, and AI driven insights by delivering scalable, high‑quality data solutions that support retail trading, operational, and customer use cases.
Build and optimise data pipelines and integrations that underpin reporting, advanced analytics, and AI/ML workloads.
Ensure analytics and reporting solutions are performant, secure, scalable, and fit for enterprise and retail decision making.

Azure & Databricks Data Engineering

Design, develop, and evolve modern data engineering solutions on Microsoft Azure, with a strong focus on Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Develop robust data transformations using PySpark, Databricks SQL, SQL, and Python.
Design, implement, and maintain curated analytical and dimensional data models aligned to retail BI, analytics, and AI requirements.

BI & Semantic Layer Delivery

Develop, optimise, and support Power BI datasets, semantic models, and reports to deliver trusted, business ready insights.
Administer Power BI environments, including workspace management, refresh orchestration, security, and access controls.
Align BI solutions to best practice data modelling, performance optimisation, and usability standards.

Production Support & Operational Excellence

Provide BAU support across data platforms, pipelines, BI, and analytics solutions, including issue resolution and root cause analysis.
Monitor data platforms and pipelines, implementing logging, alerting, and performance tuning.
Proactively identify and resolve data quality, availability, and performance issues in production environments.

Engineering Standards, DevOps & Security

Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data and analytics solutions using Azure DevOps.
Apply cloud security, authentication, and access control best practices across Azure data and analytics services.
Contribute to design reviews, code reviews, and technical discussions to ensure robust, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
Support the definition and continuous improvement of data engineering, analytics, and BI standards.

Governance & Collaboration

Contribute to the design, adoption, and communication of data governance practices across the data platform.
Partner closely with retail business stakeholders to translate requirements into reliable technical solutions.
Collaborate with engineering, data science, infrastructure, and DevOps teams to deliver secure, production ready solutions.
Balance delivery across project initiatives and ongoing BAU activities within a complex retail data environment.


What You Will Bring:


Strong hands on Data Engineering experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks SQL.
Strong experience designing and delivering data solutions on Microsoft Azure - Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Proven experience designing and building data warehouses and analytical data models, including scalable lakehouse and dimensional modelling patterns.
Strong understanding of data integration, analytics platforms, and reporting architectures in enterprise environments.
Hands‑on experience administering and developing Power BI solutions, including Dataset and semantic model design, Strong DAX capability, Performance optimisation and governance.
Experience working in DevOps environments, including - CI/CD pipelines, Source control, Release and environment management.
General understanding of cloud authentication, security, and access control concepts, including role‑based access and data security principles.
Demonstrated ability to deliver both project work and BAU/support, operating confidently in a production data and BI environment.
Proven retail sector Data Engineering experience to Senior Data Engineer level.

Why Join:

Opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.
Strong Career Development.
Amazing culture with very little red tape.

If you are a Senior Data Engineer with strong stakeholder management, who has experience in a fast-paced retail environment, and is outcome focussed we would love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

Job Title: Mid Level Software Engineer - AI Native Fintech
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role

Build the future of fintech — with AI at the core
Lendi Group is evolving into an AI-native fintech, embedding artificial intelligence into how we design, build, and operate our products. This isn’t about bolting on tools — it’s about empowering engineers to solve harder problems, move faster, and deliver smarter, more personalised experiences for millions of Australians.
We’re investing heavily in upskilling, experimentation, and modern engineering practices, giving our teams access to cutting-edge AI capabilities and the autonomy to shape how they’re used. If you’re curious, collaborative, and excited by what AI can unlock in real-world products — this is your moment.


We’re looking for a Mid Level Software Engineer to join our established team with high amount of BAU — You’ll work alongside passionate, experienced engineers tackling complex product and platform challenges, while helping define how AI is practically applied across our engineering lifecycle. You’ll have genuine influence — on architecture, engineering standards, delivery rhythm, and the evolution of an AI-enabled fintech platform — all in a team that values learning, ownership, and having fun while doing meaningful work.

Job Description


Own and deliver robust, scalable systems, influencing architecture and design decisions
Practise a true “you build it, you own it” mindset — from development to monitoring and support
Build clean, maintainable code with strong CI/CD practices and modern engineering standards

Work as a full-stack engineer, building backend services in Node.js (TypeScript) with React front-ends
Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, BAs, and Engineers in cross-functional agile squads
Partner with Product and UX to shape intuitive customer journeys and interaction patterns
Drive team rhythm across planning, delivery, deployment, and continuous improvement
Mentor and uplift engineers across the team and broader engineering group
Apply AI tools thoughtfully across the SDLC to improve productivity, quality, and outcomes

Qualifications


Core experience
5+ years of backend development experience
Strong proficiency in Node.js (TypeScript) and modern backend ecosystems
Strong API design skills: REST, schema-first design, GraphQL, webhooks, and auth (Auth0, JWT)
Deep understanding of microservices architectures and messaging patterns (event-driven, pub/sub, request-response)

Hands-on experience with Kafka, Docker, and Kubernetes
Strong AWS experience (IAM, Lambda, DynamoDB, ECS, SSM)
Experience with DevOps and Infrastructure-as-Code approaches

AI & modern engineering (new and evolving)

Strong understanding of AI tools and their practical use across the SDLC
Experience or exposure to AI engineering, including:
Building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines
Prompt engineering for effective LLM use cases
Working with LLM frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, Agno, or similar
Exposure to agentic AI (AI agents, task orchestration, automation workflows)
Willingness to work across a variety of AI tools as the ecosystem evolves

Nice to have (but not essential)

Experience with Kotlin (used in parts of our Partner Product space)
Exposure to MongoDB, including schema design, indexing, and change streams
Experience streaming or integrating data into platforms like Databricks
Analytics and observability tools such as Amplitude and Datadog
Experience with Fastify (or similar Node frameworks) and Zod schema validation
Knowledge of Auth0 + OpenFGA and multi-client authentication flows
Understanding of security and compliance in an AI-enabled environment

Why Lendi Group?
Build real products used by millions, not experiments in isolation

Be part of a genuine AI-native transformation, not just “AI theatre”
Learn, experiment, and lead with modern AI and cloud technologies
Work with smart, supportive engineers who value ownership and craft
Shape the future of fintech while continuing to grow your own skills

If you’re excited by AI-enabled engineering, ownership at scale, and building meaningful fintech products — we’d love to hear from you.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

#lendigroup #LI-AMH1 #LI-hybrid

At Snowflake, we are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.

We are looking for world-class solution engineers to join our field teams whose technical skills and customer savvy will help customers understand and utilize the value of the cutting-edge data platform that we are building.

The Solution Engineer will work hand-in-hand with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Marketing. She/he will be responsible for providing the technical expertise to make Snowflake customers successful. This solution engineer will have a broad range of skills and experience ranging from data architecture to ETL/ELT, security, performance analysis, analytics, etc. He/she will have the insight to make the connection between a customer’s specific business problems and Snowflake’s solution, the customer-facing skills to communicate that connection and vision to a wide variety of technical and executive audiences, and the technical skills to be able to not only build demos and execute proof-of-concepts but also to provide consultative assistance on architecture and implementation.

The person we’re looking for shares our passion about reinventing the data platform and thrives in a dynamic environment. That means having the flexibility and willingness to jump in and get done what needs to be done to make Snowflake and our customers successful. It means keeping up to date on the ever-evolving technologies for data and analytics in order to be an authoritative resource for both Snowflake and customers. And it means working collaboratively with a broad range of people both inside and outside the company.

RESPONSIBILITIES:


Present Snowflake technology and vision to executives and technical contributors at prospects and customers

Work hands-on with prospects and customers to demonstrate and communicate the value of Snowflake technology throughout the sales cycle, from demo to proof of concept to design and implementation

Maintain a deep understanding of competitive and complementary technologies and vendors and how to position Snowflake in relation to them

Collaborate with Product Management, Engineering, and Marketing to continuously improve Snowflake’s products and marketing

MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:


Minimum 10 years of experience working with customers in a technical pre-sales role

Outstanding skills presenting to both technical and executive audiences, whether impromptu on a whiteboard or using presentations and demos

Understanding of complete data stack and workflow, from ETL to data platform design to BI and analytics tools

Strong skills in databases, data warehouses, and data processing

Hands-on expertise with SQL and SQL analytics

STRONGLY DESIRED:


Strong Data Science/AI/ML experience across a variety of platforms

Experience and track record of success selling data and/or analytics software to enterprise customers; includes proven skills identifying key stakeholders, winning value propositions, and compelling events

Data Science knowledge and experience

Extensive knowledge of and experience with large-scale database technology (e.g. Netezza, Exadata, Teradata, Greenplum, etc.)

Software development experience with C/C++ or Java

Scripting experience with Python, Ruby, Perl, Bash

University degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics or related fields, or equivalent experience

ADDED BONUS FOR:


Experience with non-relational platforms and tools for large-scale data processing (e.g. Hadoop, HBase, etc)

Familiarity and experience with common BI and data exploration tools (e.g. Microstrategy, Business Objects, Tableau, etc)

Experience and understanding of large-scale infrastructure-as-a-service platforms (e.g. Amazon AWS, Microsoft Azure, OpenStack, etc)

Experience implementing ETL pipelines using custom and packaged tools

Experience using AWS services such as S3, Kinesis, Elastic MapReduce, Data Pipeline

Experience selling enterprise SaaS software

Proven success at enterprise software start-ups

Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.
